steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what can most reasonably be concluded about krill consumption between the two whale species. Pull the relevant numbers from the passage: the northern right whale "can consume as much as 24,000 kg of krill per day." The pygmy right whale has a maximum mass of 3,500 kg; baleen whales eat "up to 30 percent of their total mass per day," so its daily maximum is 3,500 × 0.30 = 1,050 kg.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'northern right whales have made it difficult for pygmy right whales to find food': the passage says nothing about one species affecting the other's food supply. ✗
  • Choice B — 'both whales can eat as much as 24,000 kg per day': 24,000 kg is specific to the northern right whale; the pygmy right whale's maximum is ~1,050 kg. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the northern right whale eats more krill per day than the pygmy right whale': 24,000 kg vs. ~1,050 kg — directly supported by the passage's figures. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the pygmy right whale is less likely than the northern right whale to eat krill': both are baleen whales that feed on krill; the passage makes no such distinction in likelihood. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
